title: Performance, Load, Supply-Chain Gates, and Production Client Defense · Go Client Systems · Language Lens: Load Fairness Overload
summary: A production Go integration must defend latency, memory, concurrency, dependency provenance, rollback, and behavior under doubled load. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go load fairness overload increment in a Go 1.26 net/http production HTTP client release dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test concurrency, fairness, quotas, backpressure, retry amplification, and graceful overload under changed demand. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Profile allocations, buffering, decoding, connection reuse, and response retention with representative sizes. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Load Fairness Overload · Theory Load Fairness Overload
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model release G-5 doubles traffic and body size while p95 latency, memory, retries, and queue depth rise. Measure the bottleneck, preserve behavior, verify supply-chain gates and rollback, then defend production readiness.
